openmeter/progressmanager/adapter.purpose: Redis-backed implementation of progressmanager.Adapter that stores/retrieves progress data as JSON with TTL expiration. Also provides a noop implementation (adapterNoop) and a testify-based mock (MockProgressManager) in the same package.

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() before construction
patterns.description: All required fields (Redis client, Logger, Expiration > 0) are validated in Config.Validate() before the adapter is constructed. New() returns (Adapter, error) and calls Validate() first.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (progressmanager.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err } ... }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: Both adapter structs assert interface compliance at compile time via blank identifier assignment.

patterns.name: Namespaced Redis key format
patterns.description: Keys follow the format '<keyPrefix>:progress:<namespace>:<id>' (or 'progress:<namespace>:<id>' when keyPrefix is empty). Never store progress without namespace isolation.

patterns.name: redis.Nil sentinel mapped to GenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: When redis.Get returns redis.Nil, return models.NewGenericNotFoundError(...) — not a raw error — so the HTTP layer maps it to 404.
patterns.example: if cmd.Err() == redis.Nil { return nil, models.NewGenericNotFoundError(...) }

patterns.name: Input validation before Redis ops
patterns.description: Both GetProgress and UpsertProgress call input.Validate() and wrap the error before touching Redis.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("validate get progress input: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Noop UpsertProgress silently succeeds
patterns.description: adapterNoop.UpsertProgress returns nil (no-op), but adapterNoop.GetProgress returns GenericNotFoundError. This asymmetry is intentional — writes are discarded, reads report not-found.

patterns.name: Mock implements Service not Adapter
patterns.description: MockProgressManager implements progressmanager.Service (not Adapter), covering the full service surface including DeleteProgressByRuntimeID.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config struct, New() constructor, adapter struct, and adapterNoop stub. Entry point for wiring.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.Validate() must reject nil Redis client and zero Expiration — never relax these guards.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GetProgress and UpsertProgress on the real adapter using Redis GET/SET with JSON marshaling.
key_file_guides.watch_for: getKey() is the sole key-format authority; if key format changes, all existing Redis data becomes unreachable.

key_file_guides.role: No-op implementation for disabled progress tracking; GetProgress always returns not-found, UpsertProgress is silent.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not make noop methods return non-nil errors for UpsertProgress — callers treat the noop as safe to ignore.

key_file_guides.role: Testify mock for progressmanager.Service used in unit tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mock implements Service, not Adapter — keep in sync if Service interface gains new methods.

anti_patterns: Storing progress keys without namespace prefix — breaks multi-tenant isolation
Returning raw redis errors instead of models.GenericNotFoundError for redis.Nil — breaks HTTP 404 mapping
Calling redis operations without first calling input.Validate() — bypasses invariant checks
Editing the key format in getKey() without a migration plan — orphans all existing in-flight progress records
Making adapterNoop.UpsertProgress return an error — callers rely on noop writes being safe no-ops

decisions.decision: Redis (not PostgreSQL/Ent) as the backing store
decisions.rationale: Progress data is transient and TTL-bound; Redis SET with expiration avoids schema migrations and naturally garbage-collects stale progress without a cleanup job.

decisions.decision: Noop adapter co-located with the real adapter in the same package
decisions.rationale: Keeps the disabled-feature code path trivially discoverable without a separate package; follows the broader project pattern of noop implementations per optional feature.

code_examples.scenario: Wire the Redis adapter in app/common

openmeter/progressmanager/entity.purpose: Defines all value types and input structs for the progressmanager domain: ProgressID, Progress, GetProgressInput, and UpsertProgressInput. Every type implements Validate() with errors.Join for multi-error aggregation.

patterns.name: Validate() on every domain type using errors.Join
patterns.description: Each struct has a Validate() method that collects all validation failures into a []error slice and returns errors.Join(...). Never return on first error.

patterns.name: Delegate validation to embedded types
patterns.description: Composite types call Validate() on embedded structs first, wrapping with fmt.Errorf for context. ProgressID validates NamespacedModel; GetProgressInput delegates to ProgressID.
patterns.example: if err := a.ProgressID.Validate(); err != nil { errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("progress id: %w", err)) }

patterns.name: Invariant enforcement in Progress.Validate()
patterns.description: Progress enforces business invariants: Success+Failed <= Total, and non-zero counts require non-zero Total.
patterns.example: if a.Success+a.Failed > a.Total { errs = append(errs, errors.New("success and failed must be less than or equal to total")) }

patterns.name: Input types embed domain types directly
patterns.description: GetProgressInput embeds ProgressID; UpsertProgressInput embeds Progress. Input types are thin validation wrappers, not separate structs.

key_file_guides.role: Single file for all entity types: ProgressID, Progress, GetProgressInput, UpsertProgressInput. Source of truth for domain invariants.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new fields to Progress requires updating Validate() to enforce any new invariants; JSON tags must match the API schema.

anti_patterns: Returning on first validation error instead of collecting all errors with errors.Join
Adding persistence or Redis logic to entity types — this package is pure domain types only
Omitting UpdatedAt zero-check in Progress.Validate() — callers rely on this guard before persisting

decisions.decision: Entity types in a separate sub-package (entity/) rather than the root progressmanager package
decisions.rationale: Avoids import cycles: adapter and httpdriver both need the entity types but neither should import the other; a shared entity sub-package breaks the cycle.

openmeter/progressmanager/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP handler layer for the progressmanager domain, implementing a single GetProgress endpoint. Follows the httptransport.Handler[Req, Resp] pattern with namespace resolution from context.

patterns.name: Handler interface composes sub-interfaces
patterns.description: The top-level Handler interface embeds ProgressHandler (which exposes GetProgress()). New types of endpoints add a new sub-interface and embed it in Handler.
patterns.example:
type Handler interface { ProgressHandler }
type ProgressHandler interface { GetProgress() GetProgressHandler }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion on handler struct
patterns.description: var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) ensures the concrete struct satisfies the Handler interface at compile time.

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder
patterns.description: All handlers call h.resolveNamespace(ctx) which reads the namespace from the request context via NamespaceDecoder. Returns HTTP 500 if namespace is missing.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for path-parameter endpoints
patterns.description: Endpoints with URL path arguments use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs[Req, Resp, Arg]. The first func decodes (ctx, *http.Request, arg) -> (Req, error); the second is the operation.

patterns.name: Response type aliased from api package
patterns.description: GetProgressResponse is aliased to api.Progress (the generated OpenAPI type), not a local struct. Conversion from domain type is done via a local progressToAPI() helper.
patterns.example:
type GetProgressResponse = api.Progress
func progressToAPI(p entity.Progress) api.Progress { ... }

patterns.name: HandlerOption propagation with httptransport.AppendOptions
patterns.description: Handler-level options (e.g., error encoders) are propagated to each endpoint via httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("...")).

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Handler/ProgressHandler interfaces, the handler struct, New() constructor, and resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: resolveNamespace returns HTTP 500 (not 401/403) when namespace is missing — this is intentional for self-hosted deployments using StaticNamespaceDecoder.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GetProgress endpoint: decoding, operation (calls service.GetProgress), and response encoding. Contains the progressToAPI() conversion helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: nil progress after a successful service call is treated as an internal error (not not-found) — the guard prevents silent data loss.

anti_patterns: Calling the domain service directly from the decoder function — decode must only extract request fields, the operation func calls the service
Returning domain entity types directly as HTTP responses — always convert via a local toAPI() helper using the generated api package types
Hardcoding the namespace string instead of using h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Adding business logic to progressToAPI() — it must be a pure field mapping with no validation or computation

decisions.decision: GetProgressResponse aliased to api.Progress rather than a new struct
decisions.rationale: The generated api.Progress is the contract-stable API type; aliasing avoids a redundant intermediate struct and ensures the HTTP response always matches the OpenAPI spec.

code_examples.scenario: Register the handler in the v1 router
